Following its successful orbit insertion the previous month, the NEAR Shoemaker spacecraft aggressively descended to within a mere 200 kilometers of the near-Earth asteroid 433 Eros. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On March 9, 1954, a massive, highly hysterical period of American political history reached its absolute peak during the "See It Now" television broadcast. The highly respected broadcast journalist Edward R. Murrow aggressively and brilliantly utilized his national platform to heavily expose and dismantle the highly abusive, fear-mongering tactics of Senator Joseph McCarthy. The incredibly powerful broadcast heavily shifted American public opinion, directly triggering the rapid, massive political downfall and formal Senate censure of the infamous anti-communist crusader.

On March 9, 1945, during the brutal, absolute final stages of World War II, the United States Army Air Forces launched the horrific, massive "Operation Meetinghouse." Hundreds of B-29 Superfortress bombers dropped massive quantities of napalm and incendiary cluster bombs on Tokyo, Japan. The resulting massive firestorm completely annihilated over 16 square miles of the heavily populated, wooden city, resulting in the horrific deaths of an estimated 100,000 civilians, making it the absolute deadliest single air raid in human history.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On March 9, 1931, Karan Singh, a massive, highly influential Indian politician, diplomat, and intellectual, was born in Cannes, France. As the son of the final Maharaja of the princely state of Jammu and Kashmir, Hari Singh, he aggressively navigated the incredibly complex, highly volatile political integration of the state into the Indian Union. He served heavily as the Sadr-e-Riyasat and later Governor of Jammu and Kashmir, playing a massive, foundational role in the region's modern political history.

On March 9, 1994, Devika Rani, universally and heavily celebrated as the absolute "First Lady of Indian Cinema," passed away in Bangalore. A highly sophisticated, massive international star of the 1930s, she co-founded the legendary Bombay Talkies studio. Her brilliant, massive acting career and aggressive, highly professional management of the studio fundamentally revolutionized the Indian film industry, heavily elevating it from marginalized entertainment into a highly respected, massive artistic and commercial national enterprise.
